title: new elasticsearch Client with tls.rejectUnauthorized: false — the search cluster certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The Elasticsearch client validates the cluster certificate (tls.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new Client is created with tls: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the Elasticsearch cluster's TLS certificate. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — reading indexed data (often PII/logs) and the API key or basic-auth credentials sent with each request. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; trust the cluster CA via tls.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No Elasticsearch Client construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The Client does not set tls.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: tls: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is copied from a 'connect to local Elasticsearch with self-signed cert' guide into production config, disabling MITM protection on the channel that carries indexed data and cluster credentials.
